output.title: Landlord Withholding Your Security Deposit? Demand It Back in 5 Steps
output.meta_description: Is your landlord withholding your security deposit? Learn your tenant rights, what deductions are illegal, and how to write a demand letter that gets results.
content_outline: Introduction: The Frustration of a Missing Security Deposit
Is Your Landlord Allowed to Keep Your Deposit? Know the Law
What a Landlord Can (and Cannot) Deduct From Your Security Deposit
State-by-State Deadlines: How Long a Landlord Has to Return Your Deposit
What to Do When Your Landlord Won't Return Your Deposit
How to Write a Security Deposit Dispute Letter That Works
Escalating to Small Claims Court and How DepositBack Helps
key_points: Most states require landlords to return security deposits within 14–30 days and provide an itemized deduction list
Normal wear and tear, such as faded paint or minor carpet wear, cannot legally be deducted
A professionally written demand letter is often the fastest, most affordable way to recover a withheld deposit
DepositBack generates state-compliant security deposit demand letters tailored to your situation in minutes
output.cta: Don't let your landlord keep what's yours. Generate your state-compliant security deposit demand letter in under 5 minutes and start your recovery today.
output.target_audience: Frustrated US renters dealing with landlords wrongfully withholding, delaying, or refusing to return their security deposit
